name: FileExecutableDetected
definition: Sysmon Event ID 29 records creation of a new executable file.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon emits this event when it detects creation of a new PE-format executable file. Microsoft documents Event ID 29 as FileExecutableDetected.

key_fields.explanation: The newly detected executable file. PE files under C:\\Users\\, C:\\ProgramData\\, C:\\Windows\\Temp, or AppData are higher-risk staging locations than managed install paths.
Identifies the process that created the executable and provides a stable join key to Sysmon Event ID 1 for command-line and parent-process context.
Use configured hash and signature fields, when present, to triage whether the executable is known-good, unsigned, or newly observed.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.
detection_notes: Alert when TargetFilename is a PE executable under C:\\Users\\, C:\\ProgramData\\, C:\\Windows\\Temp, or AppData and the creating Image is a browser, script interpreter, archive utility, or remote-management process. Microsoft documents Event 29 as creation of a new PE-format executable file; MITRE T1105 covers adversaries transferring tools or files into a compromised environment. Correlate ProcessGuid to Event 1, inspect hashes/signature if available, and look for immediate Sysmon Event 1 execution or Event 3 network activity from the new file.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 29
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=29 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 01:00:00.000
TargetFilename: C:\\ProgramData\\stage.exe
Image: C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe
ProcessGuid: {REDACTED}
Hashes: SHA256=REDACTED

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 29
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 29
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the documented fields.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 29
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=29
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Add the documented event-specific conditions after field validation.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 29
assumptions: Confirm the Sysmon provider because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw fields because parsed names vary.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 29
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 29
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 29
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.

value_references.meaning: Sysmon detected creation of a new PE-format executable.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The new executable path requiring origin, hash and execution review.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret TargetFilename with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 29 mean?
faqs.answer: Sysmon detected creation of a new PE-format executable.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 29 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Installers, compilers and updates create executables legitimately.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 29 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Inspect TargetFilename, creator Image, hash and signature context and correlate subsequent execution and network activity.
